requirements.statement: Deleting the derived index and rebuilding from the spec location plus repo tags produces an identical result; nothing lives only in the DB.
requirements.why: Canonical truth is the git spec location — the DB is derived and owns nothing (Invariant #1).

requirements.statement: Coverage is a SQL VIEW projected from tags — never authored, never materialized.
requirements.why: A projection cannot drift from the tags it describes (Invariant #3).

requirements.statement: Structural integrity (DUP_ID, BROKEN_SUPERSEDE, BAD_STATUS) is validated against the parsed spec at index time, never enforced as DB constraints.
requirements.why: The DB is not the source of truth; constraints there would imply it owns the spec (Invariant #4).

requirements.statement: CLI and webapp read through one shared storage interface and schema types; the webapp never opens the DB or the filesystem itself.
requirements.why: One engine, not two — forked logic drifts between surfaces (Invariant #5).

requirements.statement: A repo is drifted only when it is pinned to a spec version older than the version in which a requirement it actually references changed; behind-but-unaffected is not drift.
requirements.why: Drift must be precise or the gate cries wolf (Invariant #6).

requirements.statement: spec index walks the spec location plus member repos and (re)builds the derived SQLite index, reporting build_id and row counts.
requirements.why: Local multi-repo indexing is the substrate every other command reads.

requirements.statement: spec check reports cross-repo integrity diagnostics (dangling, superseded-referenced, orphan, unverified, drift) and exits non-zero on error-severity rows under --ci.
requirements.why: The gate; it catches all the planted mess.

requirements.statement: spec map renders the cross-repo requirement-to-repo coverage matrix derived from tags, with per-cell src/test/src+test states.
requirements.why: The platform coverage view a single-repo scan cannot give.

requirements.statement: spec propagation shows, for a superseded requirement, which repos migrated to the successor and which are still pinned behind.
requirements.why: The value moment — BILLING-009 shipped: api migrated, mobile still on BILLING-001.

requirements.statement: spec query answers full-text retrieval over requirement text and rationale via FTS5.
requirements.why: Rung-1 agent retrieval — a semantic-ish hit a plain grep for the id would miss.

requirements.statement: spec resolve maps file paths to the requirements tagged in them, with coverage.
requirements.why: Routes a task to its requirements (agent routing).

requirements.statement: spec serve launches the local webapp over the derived index, bound to loopback only.
requirements.why: CLI + DB + local webapp shape; loopback is the T-1-01 security posture.

requirements.statement: spec gate passes only when the referenced requirement is approved (Active) in the spec location and the member's pin covers its changed_at_version; failures exit 1 with a typed reason.
requirements.why: The rung-3 approval primitive — the spec merge is the approval, the PR check is the enforcement.

requirements.statement: SPEC.json parses as a domain envelope of requirements with typed statuses (active, superseded, draft, retired); malformed statuses and structurally-invalid files surface as BAD_STATUS / INVALID_DOMAIN_FILE rather than crashes.
requirements.why: The authoring and interchange format the engine ingests; silent misparses lose requirements.

requirements.statement: The tag scanner finds @spec tags anywhere in a line, derives implements/verifies from the file path, and recognizes the optional unit/integration/e2e level token.
requirements.why: Tags are how code claims a requirement; kind-by-path keeps tags one token long.

requirements.statement: spec check --ci builds fresh and is correct without trusting any cached or warm index.
requirements.why: Speed optimizations must never make correctness depend on a stale index (Invariant #2).

requirements.statement: All three adoption rungs run end to end: a lone repo self-consumes (rung 1), check --ci gates tags-bind-tests (rung 2), and the approval gate blocks superseded references (rung 3).
requirements.why: The Linear-model adoption story is the product thesis; each rung must stay demonstrable.
